content.full_text:
July 11, 1991

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, 8th Floor
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Legal Division:

The El Dorado Builders Exchange in Placerville would like to sponsor a bone marrow drive in response to the news of Supervisor John Upton's daughter having leukemia.

They have recently contacted my office for analysis, and I would like to request your written opinion in response to this request. There is an urgency to their time frame, so if you could take this into consideration I would like to hear from you as soon as possible.

The Sacramento Blood Bank would conduct the marrow drive. They will be absorbing all of the typing and operational costs. The Builders Exchange will publicize the event via press release in their industry newsletter, the County will likely do the same. There are no foreseeable costs to be incurred by the Exchange. All donations of blood will be made available internationally, although the idea first arose in response to the news of Supervisor Upton's daughter, Julie.

I would like your written legal interpretation of this marrow drive from the Builders Exchange at your earliest convenience. I am at your disposal for further information, or you may call the Sacramento Blood Bank, Marrow Department, 456-1500. Ask for Sharon.

Thank you. I'll look forward to reading your response.

Regards,

Judi Harkins,
Supervisor's Assistant
District V
